heading: Coordination of collection of domestic service employment taxes with collection of income taxes
label: 3510 Coordination of collection of domestic service employment taxes with collection of income taxes

children.text: returns with respect to domestic service employment taxes shall be made on a calendar year basis,
any such return for any calendar year shall be filed on or before the 15th day of the fourth month following the close of the employer’s taxable year which begins in such calendar year, and
no requirement to make deposits (or to pay installments under section 6157) shall apply with respect to such taxes.

children.heading: Domestic service employment taxes subject to estimated tax provisions
children.label: b Domestic service employment taxes subject to estimated tax provisions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Solely for purposes of section 6654, domestic service employment taxes imposed with respect to any calendar year shall be treated as a tax imposed by chapter 2 for the taxable year of the employer which begins in such calendar year.

children.heading: Employers not otherwise required to make estimated payments
children.label: 2 Employers not otherwise required to make estimated payments
A
children.text: no credit for wage withholding is allowed under section 31 to such employer for the taxable year of the employer which begins in such calendar year, and

children.label: B
children.text: no addition to tax would (but for this section) be imposed under section 6654 for such taxable year by reason of section 6654(e).

children.heading: Annualization
children.label: 3 Annualization
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, appropriate adjustments shall be made in the application of section 6654(d)(2) in respect of the amount treated as tax under paragraph (1).
any taxes imposed by chapter 21 or 23 on remuneration paid for domestic service in a private home of the employer, and
any amount withheld from such remuneration pursuant to an agreement under section 3402(p).

children.heading: Exception where employer liable for other employment taxes
children.label: d Exception where employer liable for other employment taxes
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, this section shall not apply to any employer for any calendar year if such employer is liable for any tax under this subtitle with respect to remuneration for services other than domestic service in a private home of the employer.

children.heading: General regulatory authority
children.label: e General regulatory authority
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section. Such regulations may treat domestic service employment taxes as taxes imposed by chapter 1 for purposes of coordinating the assessment and collection of such employment taxes with the assessment and collection of domestic employers’ income taxes.

children.heading: Authority to enter into agreements to collect State unemployment taxes
children.label: f Authority to enter into agreements to collect State unemployment taxes

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary is hereby authorized to enter into an agreement with any State to collect, as the agent of such State, such State’s unemployment taxes imposed on remuneration paid for domestic service in a private home of the employer. Any taxes to be collected by the Secretary pursuant to such an agreement shall be treated as domestic service employment taxes for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Transfers to State account
children.label: 2 Transfers to State account
children.text: Any amount collected under an agreement referred to in paragraph (1) shall be transferred by the Secretary to the account of the State in the Unemployment Trust Fund.

children.heading: Subtitle F made applicable
children.label: 3 Subtitle F made applicable
children.text: For purposes of subtitle F, any amount required to be collected under an agreement under paragraph (1) shall be treated as a tax imposed by chapter 23.

children.heading: State
children.label: 4 State
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “State” has the meaning given such term by section 3306(j)(1).
